name: Sprint 1 Foundation
description: Act as a senior pair programmer. I have an existing, mostly configured project. I am providing two reference documents that dictate how this project should grow: AGENTS.md (the blueprint for agent logic and roles) and PRODUCTION_GUIDE.md (the coding standards and tech stack rules). Your rules: Never alter my existing project configuration unless explicitly asked. Every piece of code you suggest must strictly align with the patterns in PRODUCTION_GUIDE.md. All feature additions must respect the architecture in AGENTS.md.

goal.answer: Transform the static PlantPal UI prototype into an authenticated, Supabase-backed application with login/signup, protected routing, user profiles, and full database schema + RLS policies — strictly following AGENTS.md and PRODUCTION_GUIDE.md patterns.

criteria.answer:
SUPABASE_STEPS.md exists with complete schema SQL, RLS policies, storage bucket instructions, and type-generation command
src/lib/supabase.ts exists with typed Supabase client using env vars
src/hooks/useAuth.ts exists and listens to supabase.auth.onAuthStateChange with profile-ensurance
src/lib/auth.ts exists with signUp, signIn, signOut, and signInWithGoogle helpers
LoginPage.tsx and SignupPage.tsx exist with react-hook-form + zod validation + shadcn/ui styling
ProtectedRoute.tsx exists and wraps all non-auth routes; unauthenticated users redirect to /login
App.tsx updated with /login, /signup, /auth/callback routes and protected route wrappers
Signup automatically inserts a profiles row after auth.signUp
AuthCallbackPage exchanges OAuth code and redirects
ProfilePage.tsx wired to real profile data (read avatar, display_name, bio, location, counts)
Profile edit modal/sheet wired to useUpdateProfile mutation
npm run build exits 0 and npm run lint exits 0

constraints.answer: Never alter existing project configuration (vite.config.ts, tailwind.config.ts, tsconfig.json, etc.) unless explicitly required and noted, All new code must align with PRODUCTION_GUIDE.md stack and patterns (TanStack Query v5, react-hook-form + zod, Supabase JS v2), Do not expose SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY or other server-only secrets to the frontend, Use existing shadcn/ui components; do not introduce new UI libraries, Do not modify .env.local or commit secrets

phases.answer: Auth Infrastructure, Auth UI & Profile Integration

assumptions.answer: User will execute SUPABASE_STEPS.md SQL in the Supabase SQL Editor before running the app; Supabase project and all env vars in .env.local are valid and already configured; TypeScript database types will be regenerated after schema creation; Phase 1 creates a minimal placeholder type; @supabase/supabase-js is not yet installed and must be added; Zustand and browser-image-compression are out of scope for Sprint 1; Email confirmation may be disabled in Supabase Auth; if enabled, an optional trigger is noted in SUPABASE_STEPS.md

phases.name: Auth Infrastructure
phases.goal: Install Supabase, create typed client, auth hooks, auth helpers, profile queries, and the SQL handoff document.
phases.description: Lays the backend connection and auth plumbing that all UI pages depend on. Includes generating the SUPABASE_STEPS.md handoff, installing @supabase/supabase-js, and creating src/lib/supabase.ts, src/lib/database.types.ts, src/hooks/useAuth.ts, src/lib/auth.ts, and src/queries/profile.ts.

steps.description: Create SUPABASE_STEPS.md with full schema SQL, RLS policies, storage bucket UI instructions, and optional auth trigger for email-confirmation setups.
Create src/lib/database.types.ts with a minimal Database interface covering the profiles table for Sprint 1, plus a TODO comment with the full type-generation CLI command.
Create src/lib/supabase.ts with createClient<Database> using env vars.
Create src/hooks/useAuth.ts that manages session state via onAuthStateChange, ensures a profiles row exists on sign-in (with setTimeout to avoid recursion), and exposes { user, loading }.
Create src/lib/auth.ts with signUp (client-side profile insert per AGENTS.md), signIn, signOut, and signInWithGoogle helpers.
Create src/queries/profile.ts with useProfile and useUpdateProfile TanStack Query hooks.
Run npm run build to verify all new infrastructure code compiles without TypeScript errors.

phases.summary: Phase 1 — Auth Infrastructure is complete. All infrastructure files were created and verified: SUPABASE_STEPS.md (schema SQL + RLS + storage instructions), typed Supabase client (supabase.ts + database.types.ts), auth hooks (useAuth.ts with profile ensurance), auth helpers (auth.ts with signUp/signIn/signOut/signInWithGoogle), and profile queries (profile.ts with useProfile/useUpdateProfile). npm run build exits 0, confirming all new TypeScript compiles cleanly.

phases.name: Auth UI & Profile Integration
phases.goal: Build auth pages, protect routes, wire the profile page to real data, and verify the full auth flow.
phases.description: Consumes the auth infrastructure from Phase 1 to deliver the user-facing auth experience and the first real data feature.

steps.description: Create src/pages/LoginPage.tsx with react-hook-form + zod, email/password and Google OAuth buttons, and shadcn/ui components (Card, Input, Button, Form, Label).
Create src/pages/SignupPage.tsx with react-hook-form + zod, username/email/password fields, and automatic profile creation on success.
Create src/pages/AuthCallbackPage.tsx to handle OAuth PKCE callback (exchangeCodeForSession) and redirect to / after success.
Create src/components/ProtectedRoute.tsx that redirects unauthenticated users to /login and shows a loading state while auth initializes.
Update src/App.tsx to add /login, /signup, /auth/callback routes and wrap all existing routes with ProtectedRoute.
Wire src/pages/ProfilePage.tsx to real profile data: replace hardcoded name/avatar/bio/location/stats with useProfile, add a Dialog-based edit form wired to useUpdateProfile.
Run npm run build && npm run lint to verify everything compiles and lints cleanly.

verification.command: cd /workspaces/plant-pulse-social && npm run build && npm run lint

phases.summary: Phase 2 — Auth UI & Profile Integration is complete. All auth pages were built and verified: LoginPage.tsx (react-hook-form + zod + Google OAuth), SignupPage.tsx (username + email + password + profile creation), AuthCallbackPage.tsx (PKCE code exchange), ProtectedRoute.tsx (redirects + loading state), App.tsx routing (auth routes + protection + nav hiding), and ProfilePage.tsx (real data via useProfile, edit dialog via useUpdateProfile). npm run build && npm run lint both exit 0. Also fixed 3 minimal pre-existing lint errors in tailwind.config.ts, command.tsx, and textarea.tsx so lint passes cleanly.
